name: python-nmap (Nmap Scanner Python Wrapper)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL); Alexandre Norman; requires nmap binary

auth.notes: Uses local nmap binary; requires root/sudo for OS detection and SYN scans

performance.notes: Network-dependent; scan speed controlled by -T0/-T5 timing templates

agent_readiness.notes: Python wrapper for nmap network scanner. nm = nmap.PortScanner(). nm.scan('192.168.1.0/24', '22-443'). Results: nm['host']['tcp'][22]['state']. Arguments pass through to nmap (-sV, -O, -A, -T4, etc.). Async scanning via PortScannerAsync. XML output parsing. Host discovery, port states, service detection, OS detection. Requires nmap binary (not pure Python). For authorized penetration testing and network auditing only.

gotchas: Root/sudo required for SYN scans and OS detection
Use only on authorized networks — unauthorized scanning is illegal
Thin wrapper — nmap syntax knowledge still needed
best_when: Authorized network discovery and port scanning in security audits
avoid_when: Cloud environments where nmap may trigger security alerts
summary: python-nmap is a Python wrapper for the nmap network scanner. PortScanner().scan(host, ports) with full nmap argument passthrough (-sV, -O, OS detection). Async scanning support. Host discovery, service detection, OS fingerprinting. Requires nmap binary and often root/sudo. Use only on authorized networks for penetration testing and security audits.
